concepts_list.name: density
concepts_list.description: Fluid density, appearing in the momentum equation and viscosity dependence
concepts_list.justification: Explicitly denoted as 'rho' in the text, and referenced in the balance of linear momentum equation as the fluid density.

concepts_list.name: viscosity
concepts_list.description: Dynamic viscosity of the fluid, which may depend on the shear rate and is central to the generalised Newtonian fluid model
concepts_list.justification: Explicitly denoted as 'mu' in the text, and described as a parameter that may depend on the shear rate in the Carreau model.

concepts_list.name: velocity_field
concepts_list.description: Fluid velocity field, denoted by u, which is the primary unknown in the momentum and continuity equations
concepts_list.justification: Referenced multiple times as 'u' in the momentum and continuity equations, and appears in both the strong form and weak form of the problem.

concepts_list.name: pressure_field
concepts_list.description: Fluid pressure field, denoted by p, which is coupled with velocity through the continuity equation and stabilised in the finite element formulation
concepts_list.justification: Explicitly denoted as 'p' in the continuity equation and appears in the weak form as a variable to be solved for.

concepts_list.name: test_velocity
concepts_list.description: Test function for velocity, denoted by w_h, used in the weak form of the momentum equation
concepts_list.justification: Appears as 'w_h' in the weak formulation of the momentum equation, with subscript 'h' indicating finite element discretisation.

concepts_list.name: test_pressure
concepts_list.description: Test function for pressure, denoted by q_h, used in the pressure stabilisation term of the weak form
concepts_list.justification: Appears as 'q_h' in the weak form of the pressure equation, used to enforce the pressure-stabilised Petrov-Galerkin condition.

concepts_list.name: boundary_normal_vector
concepts_list.description: Unit outward normal vector on the boundary, denoted by n, used in the boundary traction condition
concepts_list.justification: Explicitly referenced as 'n' in the boundary condition equation (7), and used in the normal traction term involving the stress tensor.

concepts_list.name: shear_rate_operator
concepts_list.description: Gradient operator applied to the velocity field, denoted by ∇_s u, representing the symmetric gradient of the velocity
concepts_list.justification: Appears as 'nabla_s' in multiple equations, defined as the symmetric gradient of the velocity field, used in the expression for shear rate and viscosity.

concepts_list.name: boundary_traction
concepts_list.description: Normal traction on the boundary, denoted by t, which may be zero or prescribed to enforce boundary conditions
concepts_list.justification: Explicitly denoted as 't' in the boundary condition equation (7), and described as a pseudo-traction that may be set to zero or used to prescribe mean pressures.

concepts_list.name: domain
concepts_list.description: The spatial domain of the fluid flow, denoted by Ω, where the governing equations are defined
concepts_list.justification: Explicitly referenced as 'GREEK CAPITAL LETTER OMEGA' (Ω) in the problem statement and equations, describing the spatial region of the flow.

concepts_list.name: time_interval
concepts_list.description: The time interval over which the flow is considered, from t=0 to t=T
concepts_list.justification: Explicitly mentioned in the problem statement as the time interval from t=0 to t=T, where the flow evolution is defined.

new_concept_info.description:
Fluid density, appearing in the momentum equation and viscosity dependence
JUSTIFICATION:
Explicitly denoted as 'rho' in the text, and referenced in the balance of linear momentum equation as the fluid density.

Dynamic viscosity of the fluid, which may depend on the shear rate and is central to the generalised Newtonian fluid model
JUSTIFICATION:
Explicitly denoted as 'mu' in the text, and described as a parameter that may depend on the shear rate in the Carreau model.

Fluid velocity field, denoted by u, which is the primary unknown in the momentum and continuity equations
JUSTIFICATION:
Referenced multiple times as 'u' in the momentum and continuity equations, and appears in both the strong form and weak form of the problem.

Fluid pressure field, denoted by p, which is coupled with velocity through the continuity equation and stabilised in the finite element formulation
JUSTIFICATION:
Explicitly denoted as 'p' in the continuity equation and appears in the weak form as a variable to be solved for.

Test function for velocity, denoted by w_h, used in the weak form of the momentum equation
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears as 'w_h' in the weak formulation of the momentum equation, with subscript 'h' indicating finite element discretisation.

Unit outward normal vector on the boundary, denoted by n, used in the boundary traction condition
JUSTIFICATION:
Explicitly referenced as 'n' in the boundary condition equation (7), and used in the normal traction term involving the stress tensor.

Gradient operator applied to the velocity field, denoted by ∇_s u, representing the symmetric gradient of the velocity
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears as 'nabla_s' in multiple equations, defined as the symmetric gradient of the velocity field, used in the expression for shear rate and viscosity.

Normal traction on the boundary, denoted by t, which may be zero or prescribed to enforce boundary conditions
JUSTIFICATION:
Explicitly denoted as 't' in the boundary condition equation (7), and described as a pseudo-traction that may be set to zero or used to prescribe mean pressures.
